title: Frontier attestation in reports and handoffs
goal: Implement AF-07 from docs/roadmap/auto-frontier/08-attestation.md. Derive an auditable frontier block in reports/handoff/read models from envelope, proposal, decision, event, queue, and slice provenance records. Extend roadmap-truth-check so generated slices with provenance must reference an accepted proposal decision. Do not add a new truth store.
acceptance: Report and handoff JSON include a derived frontier block with envelope snapshot, generated-slice graph, authorizers, proposal ids, tier reason codes, budget consumption, deferred/rejected/pending fog, operator-needed stops, and shadow agreement metrics.
Runs with no frontier activity render an explicit empty state where appropriate; legacy runs remain backward-compatible.
Handoff readiness remains blocked by unresolved pending proposals and the frontier block explains those pending items.
scripts/roadmap-truth-check rejects generated slices whose provenance origin proposal is missing or not accepted, while hand-written slices without provenance still pass existing checks.
The frontier block is regeneration-equivalent from existing records; no new persisted truth store is added.
must_ask_if: If the implementation would create a second proposal, decision, or apply authority channel outside the existing replan mechanism, stop and ask.
If required runtime behavior cannot be implemented within the declared areas, stop and ask rather than widening scope silently.
If a dependency or prerequisite from docs/roadmap/auto-frontier/00-matrix.md is not satisfied, stop and report the blocker.
If attestation would require storing a second promotion graph rather than deriving it, stop and ask.
